# Evidence Brief - Health system (SDG 3): Kenya

*Generated by CEDAR on 2026-06-20T14:52:41Z | Glass-box mode: every figure is cited below.*

## Key findings

- [+] Life expectancy at birth rose from 60.9 to 63.6 years between 2010 and 2023 (+4.4%, +0.3%/yr). [1]
- [+] Maternal mortality ratio fell from 210 to 149 per 100,000 live births between 2010 and 2023 (-29.0%, -2.6%/yr). [2]
- [!] As of 2023, the value of 149 does not yet meet the benchmark of 70 (SDG 3.1: maternal mortality <70 per 100,000 by 2030); a gap of 79 per 100,000 live births remains. [3]
- [+] Under-5 mortality rate fell from 53.1 to 39.6 per 1,000 live births between 2010 and 2023 (-25.4%, -2.2%/yr). [4]
- [!] As of 2023, the value of 39.6 does not yet meet the benchmark of 25 (SDG 3.2.1: reduce under-5 mortality to at least as low as 25 per 1,000 live births by 2030); a gap of 14.6 per 1,000 live births remains. [5]

## Data confidence & caveats

- **Life expectancy at birth** - confidence: **High** (coverage 100%, latest 2023).
- **Maternal mortality ratio** - confidence: **High** (coverage 100%, latest 2023).
- **Under-5 mortality rate** - confidence: **High** (coverage 100%, latest 2023).

## Methods & reproducibility

All figures are pulled from authoritative APIs and computed deterministically (trend = first vs latest observation; CAGR = compound annual rate; gap = distance to published benchmark). No figure is model-generated. Re-run any number with the query URLs in the evidence ledger below.
